note: Markers from Wikipedia:Signs of AI writing that require semantic judgement, edit history, or network access and are NOT auto-detected. The skill lists them so the agent/author can review them manually.

manualChecks.title: Undue emphasis on significance, legacy, and broader trends
manualChecks.why: Requires reading for meaning: puffery about importance/legacy/broader context.
manualChecks.hint: Look for sentences that inflate significance or tie the subject to sweeping trends without sources.

manualChecks.title: Superficial analyses
manualChecks.why: Needs judgement about depth and substance of claims.
manualChecks.hint: Analysis that sounds comprehensive but says little concrete; often paired with rule of three.

manualChecks.title: Promotional tone beyond the flagged phrases
manualChecks.why: Concrete phrases are auto-flagged (language.promotional); overall tone still needs judgement.
manualChecks.hint: Unsourced superlatives, brochure tone, puffery not captured by phrase markers.

manualChecks.title: Unsupported attributions beyond the flagged phrases
manualChecks.why: Phrases like 'по словам экспертов' are auto-flagged (language.vague-attribution); verifying the source needs judgement.
manualChecks.hint: Check whether 'critics say' / 'experts argue' are actually supported by the cited source.

manualChecks.title: Negative parallelisms (not just X but Y / not X but Y / X rather than Y)
manualChecks.why: Reliable detection needs semantic parsing; auto-regex is too noisy.
manualChecks.hint: Watch for 'not only ... but also', 'it's not ..., it's ...', 'X rather than Y'.

manualChecks.title: Rule of three
manualChecks.why: Common in human writing too; needs judgement in context.
manualChecks.hint: Triads of adjectives/phrases used to sound comprehensive.

manualChecks.title: Lexical diversity / elegant variation
manualChecks.why: Needs judgement; non-native writers also avoid repetition.
manualChecks.hint: Synonyms swapped in to avoid repeating a key term.

manualChecks.title: Broken external links
manualChecks.why: Requires network access to verify each URL.
manualChecks.hint: Several dead links not found in the Internet Archive are a strong sign.

manualChecks.title: Invalid DOIs or DOIs leading to unrelated articles
manualChecks.hint: Plausible-looking DOI that resolves to an unrelated paper.

manualChecks.title: Book citations that do not verify the text
manualChecks.why: Requires checking the cited pages against the source.
manualChecks.hint: General book cited without URL where the cited page does not support the claim.

manualChecks.title: Non-existent categories or templates
manualChecks.why: Requires checking existence against a wiki.
manualChecks.hint: Hallucinated categories/infoboxes appearing as red links (Wikipedia-specific).

manualChecks.title: Broken wikitext / garbled templates
manualChecks.why: Wikitext-specific; not relevant to Markdown/Obsidian articles.
manualChecks.hint: Garbled AfC submission templates or mangled category timestamps.

manualChecks.title: Pronounced shift in writing style
manualChecks.hint: A section that suddenly reads very differently from the author's usual voice.
